status_detail: Phase A engine code addition (Connect-RPC branch in generate_block_response, only triggers for response_type prefix 'connect_rpc') introduced ZERO regression on chatgpt. POST /backend-anon/f/conversation returned 200 OK with 5 response headers identical to prior PASS cycles: Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true, Access-Control-Allow-Origin: https://chatgpt.com, Cache-Control, Content-Type, X-Content-Type-Options. Warning rendered correctly with text '민감정보가 포함된 요청은 보안 정책에 의해 차단되었습니다.' (with warning triangle icon in assistant chat bubble area). Comparison to #615: SAME -- identical warning text, identical response headers, identical 200 OK status, identical 'EventStream tab empty but warning rendered on page' pattern. Console errors observed are all pre-existing FedCM/Google sign-in noise (3 msgs) -- none related to APF pipeline. chatgpt_sse path unaffected by Connect-RPC code addition.
warning_text_excerpt: 민감정보가 포함된 요청은 보안 정책에 의해 차단되었습니다.
eventstream_events_seen: no (empty -- Etap injected warning into stream as non-SSE body, no SSE rows displayed; consistent with prior behavior in #615)
regression_explanation: chatgpt warning renders identically to prior PASS cycles; Phase A Connect-RPC code change had zero impact on chatgpt_sse path.
comparison_to_615: same -- identical warning text, identical response headers (ACAO/ACAC both present), identical 200 OK status, identical 'EventStream tab empty but warning rendered on page' pattern. Regression-free.
console_noise: 3 pre-existing FedCM/Google sign-in messages (GSI_LOGGER warning, FedCM disabled error, GSI_LOGGER NetworkError) -- unrelated to APF pipeline
notes: Cycle 95 R-14 -- chatgpt regression test post Phase A code change. PASS, no regression. Phase A's response_type='connect_rpc' guard correctly isolates new branch from existing chatgpt_sse path. Subagent 4m30s, within 5min soft.
